hypothesis: Augmenting the training data of a conservative uMLIP with 0.5–1.0% MOF-specific out-of-equilibrium configurations that include linker torsions and transient metal–ligand bond elongations will reduce the rate of structural optimization non-convergence at 10−3 eV/Å by at least 50% and lower the MOF bulk modulus MAE by ≥25% compared to the same architecture trained without MOF data.
